title: Intense Indian Milk Rasam
description: A luscious sour, pillowy rasam that combines the creamy richness of milk with the bracing tang of kombu cold extract, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: Bring the milk to a gentle simmer in a medium saucepan.
Add the kombu cold extract and stir until the mixture smells bracing and slightly citrusy.
Pour in the tamarind water and tomato puree, then bring to a low boil.
Reduce the heat to a gentle simmer and add lemon juice, black salt, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, dried chilies, asafoetida and turmeric.
Stir in the fresh coriander and let the rasam simmer for 5 minutes to blend the flavors.
Turn off the heat and stir in ghee, letting it melt into the sauce.
Taste and adjust salt if needed.
Serve the rasam hot in a bowl, garnished with a sprinkle of fresh coriander.
Enjoy this creamy, tangy rasam as a comforting midnight snack or light dinner.
Take care to keep the simmer low to prevent scorching the milk.
